title: Tracking website, food watch: South Korea is obsessing over Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's visit
description: South Korean investors are eagerly tracking Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's every move as they wait to see what the AI megacap company will do next.

title: South Korea stocks fall 5% as tech heavyweights follow plunge in Wall Street's AI-linked names
description: Asia-Pacific markets fell on Friday as investors assessed a rotation out of chip stocks on Wall Street that lifted the Dow Jones Industrial Average to a record close.

title: Asia tech stocks drop after Broadcom rattles AI trade and drags Wall Street names lower
description: Asian technology shares fell, tracking losses in U.S. semiconductor stocks after disappointing earnings Broadcom sparked a rotation out of AI-linked names.

title: In ‘wild’ twist, SpaceX won’t be allowed early entry to the S&P 500 after all
description: S&P Dow Jones Indices considered whether to loosen index requirements but opted to keep its policies unchanged.

title: Wall Street’s red carpet for SpaceX
description: Banks and stock exchanges have bent over backwards for the historic IPO of Elon Musk’s rocket company

title: Backlash over plan to drop US quarterly reporting demands is building
description: Enraged investors have sent flood of letters to SEC to express their opposition

title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX lines up retail investors for record IPO allocation
description: Up to a quarter of the rocket builder’s $75bn float will be set aside for individual investors

title: Oxford BioMedica leaves door open to private equity takeover
description: UK-listed group has already rejected multiple unsolicited offers from EQT and says a deal would have to be the right fit

title: Hedge funds bet against call centre stocks as AI threat grows
description: Outsourcing companies hit as investors see ‘clean’ disruption risk

title: How to take advantage of disruption
description: Established industries are facing a fresh wave of digital transformation

title: Iron Ore Futures Head for Fourth Weekly Loss on Seasonal Lull
description: Iron ore headed for a fourth straight weekly loss on a seasonally weakening Chinese market and expectations for more shipments from Australia.

title: Bitcoin Treasury Firms Shed $62 Billion in Deepening Crypto Rout
description: Bitcoin’s slide this week is adding fresh pressure to one of the most ambitious financial experiments to emerge from the recent crypto boom: publicly traded companies created to accumulate digital assets on behalf of investors.

title: Vingroup’s 1,000% Surge Tests Limits of Vietnam’s Market Boom
description: Blistering gains in Vietnam’s biggest stock have confounded seasoned analysts and set off a frenzy among retail traders, raising doubts about how long the rally can last.

title: China’s Crowded Solar Industry Pivots to New Areas of Growth
description: China’s solar industry is tacitly admitting something it has feared for years: it doesn’t know how to escape chronic overcapacity that has weighed on profits.

title: AllianzGI Is Said in Exclusive Talks to Buy UOB Asset Management
description: Allianz Global Investors is in exclusive talks to buy the asset management division of Singapore’s United Overseas Bank Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Industrial Metals Swing Lower Ahead of Crucial US Jobs Report
description: Copper slumped, and other base metals fell, as caution swept through risk assets ahead of key US employment data that could shape expectations for Federal Reserve policy.

title: ‘Sell Indonesia’ Sweeps Trading Desks as Prabowo Tightens Grip
description: Global investors are rapidly losing confidence in Indonesia as the nation’s stocks tumble at the fastest pace worldwide and its currency sinks to all-time lows.

title: Philippine Inflation Slows as Transport Cost Pressures Ease
description: Philippine inflation unexpectedly decelerated in May as easing transport costs took pressure off soaring prices triggered by the Iran war.

title: Indonesia Denies Finance Chief Purbaya to Go as Markets Slump
description: Indonesian Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa denied he is resigning from office, after rumors of his departure swept through slumping markets this week.

title: India Rate-Hike Bets Build as Pricey Oil, Weak Rupee Stoke Inflation Risks
description: Most economists expect the RBI to hold today, though an August hike looks near certain.

title: Jan de Nul Wins Contract to Dig Argentina’s $10 Billion Waterway
description: Belgian dredger Jan de Nul NV and local partner Servimagnus SA won a 25-year contract on Thursday from Argentina’s government to upgrade the nation’s chief trade route in a concession overshadowed by accusations of Chinese influence.

title: US Futures, Korean Stocks Drop
description: Korean stocks are pulling back from record highs as Broadcom Inc.’s outlook for AI-chip sales fell short of elevated expectations, pausing a blistering advance in semiconductor shares. Bloomberg's Anthony Stephens breaks down the situation. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: HSBC, AIA Slump After Report of HK Bank Account Curbs
description: HSBC Holdings Plc and AIA Group Ltd. shares fell after a media report said some banks have suspended opening Hong Kong bank accounts for clients in mainland China that could be used for overseas investments. Bloomberg's Stephen Engle has the context. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ex-Coup Boss Tightens Grip on Guinea With Parliamentary Vote Win
description: Guinean President Mamadi Doumbouya has strengthened his hold on power after his party won most of the seats in the May 31 parliamentary elections.

title: T&D Will Use Funds From Sale of Insurance Unit for Buybacks
description: T&D Holdings Inc. plans to use the roughly ¥188 billion ($1.2 billion) it will get from the sale of a life insurance subsidiary for share buybacks and strategic investments, including in artificial intelligence.

title: Broadcom's Tan on 'Leap of Faith' on AI
description: Broadcom CEO Hock Tan discusses the "leap of faith" the company took when starting its work with Anthropic on chips a year ago, describing it as a "great bet." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fuel Shocks, Rising Prices Threaten India’s Consumption Growth
description: The global energy crisis is threatening to slow spending among India’s vast middle class, undermining a fragile recovery in consumption and adding to the woes of an economy already grappling with a markets and currency slump.

title: IPOs, Huawei Plan Add to China’s $900 Billion Chip Stock Boom
description: The rally in China’s semiconductor stocks is poised to extend thanks to upcoming blockbuster IPOs and technology breakthroughs, investors and analysts say.

title: A Little-Known Indian Stock’s 530% Rally Shows Hidden AI Winners
description: The overarching narrative is that Indian equity markets missed out on the global artificial intelligence boom. But a look under the hood reveals a slew of smaller firms winning from trillions of dollars being spent on AI capacity.

title: US plans to fight flesh-eating screwworm outbreak with flies and dogs
description: The insect, which has been found in America for the first time since 1966, is a growing concern in the southern US.

title: British Heart Foundation plans to close 150 charity shops
description: The charity says it is facing "an exceptionally challenging trading environment".

title: China cracks down on soft porn, violence and materialism in viral micro dramas
description: Micro dramas have surged in popularity, but drawn criticism for often sensationalist content.

title: Trump announces $700m coal investment using wartime powers
description: The president is directing money into coal as the Iran war drives up energy costs for Americans.

title: The Priests Revered in the Land of Five Genders
description: The bissus of South Sulawesi are considered a link between the earthly and celestial because they are thought to embody both male and female traits.

title: Russia Rails Against the West but Welcomes Candace Owens and Andrew Tate
description: While some powerful Russians shun the West, others want to restore ties and embrace friendly Westerners. President Vladimir V. Putin’s annual economic conference illustrates the conflicting impulses.

title: What To Know as China’s Xi Jinping Heads to North Korea
description: As Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang, he faces an emboldened North Korean dictator, whose alliance with Russia has reduced his dependence on China.

title: House Passes Ukraine Aid in Defiance of Republican Leaders
description: Eighteen G.O.P. lawmakers broke with their party and joined Democrats to deliver yet another blow to the president’s foreign policy agenda.

title: To Fix Haiti, Look to Colombia
description: An example—albeit an imperfect one—of how to achieve a lasting peace.

title: Why Mogadishu clashes are deepening Somalia’s political crisis again
description: Tensions over Somali elections escalate into violence, leaving Mogadishu residents grappling with fear and disruption.

title: Iran war live: Hezbollah rejects truce as Israel continues Lebanon strikes
description: Israel's supreme court annuls government ban on international Red Cross visiting Palestinian prisoners in Israeli jails.

title: Global Prosperity Summit 2026 showcases Hong Kong’s role in future-focused industries and global cooperation
description: Hong Kong is embracing its growing role on the international stage in vital sectors driving global prosperity – a message highlighted at an annual gathering of international industry leaders, corporate executives and local policymakers who back the city’s upward momentum. The Global Prosperity Summit 2026 (GPS 2026) showcased high-stakes international dialogue over two days in Hong Kong last month for panel discussions on the city’s potential across several fast-evolving and globally significant...

title: Founders Fund launches game show starring Sam Altman, Palmer Luckey, and other tech elites
description: The debut episode, moderated by Founders Fund chief marketing officer Mike Solana, included a star-studded cast of current tech luminaries.

title: Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire agreed by Israel and Lebanon
description: The United States announced the ceasefire agreement on Wednesday night following a fresh round of talks.

title: How Trump's White House ballroom plan has doubled in size and cost over a year
description: BBC Verify examines how the biggest change to the White House in decades has transformed in the last year.

title: Hezbollah Rejects Cease-Fire Deal Between Lebanon and Israel
description: Hezbollah’s leader said the Iran-backed group, which was not included in U.S.-brokered talks, said a truce worked out between Israel and the Lebanese government amounted to surrender for his group.

title: Ireland, Seen as a Weak Link in Europe’s Defense, Is Trying to Bulk Up
description: As concern rises in Europe over threats from an emboldened Russia, the Irish government says it’s working to plug gaps in its military, which reflect a tradition of neutrality.

description: The Trump administration’s efforts to tighten the economic noose on Cuba appear to be working, as more international firms announced they would leave the island.

title: Tracking the Rising Death Toll From U.S. Boat Strikes
description: The New York Times has been investigating the number of boat strikes and the death toll in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific carried out by the Trump administration. Eric Schmitt, a national security correspondent for The Times, explains what those numbers mean.

title: Nick Bilton, New ‘60 Minutes’ Chief, Pledges Independence
description: Nick Bilton said he had consulted with the program’s remaining correspondents: Lesley Stahl, Jon Wertheim and Bill Whitaker. All three were deliberating whether to stay with the show, two people said.
